links.rationale: Both cite/use textual evidence to support analysis including inferences; 6.5F is a genre-neutral Comprehension SE that also anchors RI.6.1.
Genre-neutral Response SE 'use text evidence to support an appropriate response' overlaps the evidence-citing skill; also anchors RI.6.1.
Shared skill: determine/infer theme using text detail (literary-elements SE 6.7A infers themes with text evidence).
Objective-summary component of RL.6.2b maps to genre-neutral Response SE 6.6D 'paraphrase and summarize'; also relevant to RI.6.2.
Both analyze how characters respond/change to move the plot toward resolution (6.7B: internal/external responses develop plot).
Shared skill: figurative/connotative language and its effect (genre-neutral Author's-craft SE 6.9D); also anchors RI.6.4.
Impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone maps to genre-neutral 6.9F 'language contributes to mood and voice.'
How a part fits the overall structure and develops meaning maps to genre-neutral 6.9B 'text structure contributes to author's purpose.'
Both address how point of view develops narrator/speaker perspective (6.9E: omniscient/limited point of view as literary device).
Comparing texts across forms/genres on similar themes maps to genre-neutral Response SE 6.6B 'comparing sources within and across genres'; also relevant to RI.6.9.
RL.6.10's text-to-self/text/world connection clause maps to genre-neutral Comprehension SE 6.5E 'make connections to personal experiences, ideas in other texts, and society.'
Cite evidence to support analysis and inferences; genre-neutral Comprehension SE 6.5F also anchors RL.6.1.
Genre-neutral Response SE 'use text evidence to support an appropriate response'; also anchors RL.6.1.
Central idea conveyed through details maps to informational-genre SE 6.8D 'controlling idea or thesis with supporting evidence.'
Objective-summary component maps to genre-neutral Response SE 6.6D 'paraphrase and summarize.'
Determining figurative/connotative/technical word meaning overlaps genre-neutral Author's-craft SE 6.9D on figurative language; also anchors RL.6.4.
How parts fit the overall informational structure and develop ideas maps to 6.8D 'characteristics and structural elements of informational text' incl. organizational patterns.
Genre-neutral 6.9B 'text structure contributes to author's purpose' also captures the structure-analysis skill.
Both determine author's perspective/purpose and how it is conveyed (Author's-craft SE 6.9A: author's purpose and message).
Trace/evaluate argument and claims, distinguishing supported vs unsupported, maps to argumentative-genre SE 6.8E (claim + how author uses evidence).
Judging whether claims are supported by evidence overlaps 6.9G 'differences between rhetorical devices and logical fallacies.'
Comparing one author's presentation with another's maps to genre-neutral Response SE 6.6B 'comparing sources within and across genres'; also relevant to RL.6.9.
Cite several pieces of evidence to support analysis/inferences; genre-neutral Comprehension SE 7.5F also anchors RI.7.1.
Genre-neutral Response SE 'use text evidence to support an appropriate response'; also anchors RI.7.1.
Determine theme and analyze its development maps to literary-elements SE 7.7A 'infer multiple themes within and across texts using text evidence.'
Objective-summary component maps to genre-neutral Response SE 7.6D 'paraphrase and summarize.'
Analyzing how story elements interact (setting shaping character/plot) maps to literary-elements SE 7.7D 'setting influences character and plot development.'
Figurative/connotative meaning and its effect maps to genre-neutral Author's-craft SE 7.9D on figurative language; also anchors RI.7.4.
Impact of word choices on meaning and tone maps to genre-neutral 7.9F 'language contributes to mood, voice, and tone.'
RL.7.4's sound-device/rhyme-in-poetry clause maps to poetry-genre SE 7.8B 'rhyme scheme, meter, and graphical elements in poems.'
How a poem's form contributes to meaning maps to poetry-genre SE 7.8B on poetic structure/effect.
How a drama's form contributes to meaning maps to drama-genre SE 7.8C 'playwrights develop characters through dialogue and staging.'
Both analyze point of view to develop/contrast perspectives (7.9E: subjective and objective point of view as literary device).
Comparing fictional and historical accounts of a period maps to genre-neutral Response SE 7.6B 'comparing sources within and across genres'; also relevant to RI.7.9.
RL.7.10's text-to-self/text/world connection clause maps to genre-neutral Comprehension SE 7.5E 'make connections.'
Cite several pieces of evidence to support analysis/inferences; genre-neutral 7.5F also anchors RL.7.1.
Genre-neutral Response SE 'use text evidence to support an appropriate response'; also anchors RL.7.1.
Central-idea determination/development maps to informational-genre SE 7.8D 'controlling idea or thesis with supporting evidence.'
Objective-summary component maps to genre-neutral Response SE 7.6D 'paraphrase and summarize.'
Impact of specific word choice on meaning and tone maps to genre-neutral 7.9F 'language contributes to mood, voice, and tone'; also anchors RL.7.4.
Figurative/connotative/technical meaning overlaps genre-neutral Author's-craft SE 7.9D on figurative language.
Analyzing the structure an author uses and how major sections develop ideas maps to informational-genre SE 7.8D (structural elements/organizational patterns).
Genre-neutral 7.9B 'text structure contributes to author's purpose' also captures the structure-analysis skill.
Determine author's perspective/purpose maps to Author's-craft SE 7.9A 'author's purpose and message.'
Analyzing how the author distinguishes a position from others overlaps argumentative SE 7.8E (evidence + consideration of alternatives).
Trace/evaluate argument for sound reasoning and sufficient evidence maps to argumentative SE 7.8E (evidence + consideration of alternatives).
Assessing whether reasoning is sound overlaps 7.9G on rhetorical devices and logical fallacies.
Analyzing how authors on the same topic emphasize different evidence maps to genre-neutral Response SE 7.6B 'comparing sources within and across genres'; also relevant to RL.7.9.
Cite the strongest evidence to support analysis/inferences; genre-neutral Comprehension SE 8.5F also anchors RI.8.1.
Genre-neutral Response SE 'use text evidence to support an appropriate response'; also anchors RI.8.1.
Theme development in relation to character/setting/plot maps to literary-elements SE 8.7A 'how themes are developed through the interaction of characters and events.'
Objective-summary component maps to genre-neutral Response SE 8.6D 'paraphrase and summarize.'
How dialogue/incidents propel action and reveal character maps to literary-elements SE 8.7B 'characters' motivations and behaviors influence events and resolution.'
Figurative/connotative meaning and effect maps to genre-neutral Author's-craft SE 8.9D on figurative language; also anchors RI.8.4.
Impact of word choices on meaning, mood, and tone maps to genre-neutral 8.9F 'language contributes to the mood, voice, and tone.'
Comparing how differing text structure contributes to meaning/style maps to genre-neutral 8.9B 'text structure contributes to author's purpose.'
Differences in points of view creating effects like suspense/humor via dramatic irony maps to 8.9E 'multiple points of view and irony.'
RL.8.10's text-to-self/text/world connection clause maps to genre-neutral Comprehension SE 8.5E 'make connections.'
Cite the strongest evidence to support analysis/inferences; genre-neutral 8.5F also anchors RL.8.1.
Genre-neutral Response SE 'use text evidence to support an appropriate response'; also anchors RL.8.1.
Central-idea determination/development in relation to supporting ideas maps to informational-genre SE 8.8D 'controlling idea or thesis with supporting evidence.'
Objective-summary component maps to genre-neutral Response SE 8.6D 'paraphrase and summarize.'
Impact of word choices on meaning and tone maps to genre-neutral 8.9F 'language contributes to the mood, voice, and tone'; also anchors RL.8.4.
Figurative/connotative/technical meaning overlaps genre-neutral Author's-craft SE 8.9D on figurative language.
Analyzing paragraph/sentence roles in developing a key concept overlaps informational-genre SE 8.8D (structural elements, multiple organizational patterns developing the thesis).
Genre-neutral 8.9B 'text structure contributes to author's purpose' also captures the structure-analysis skill.
Determine author's purpose maps to Author's-craft SE 8.9A 'author's purpose and message.'
How the author acknowledges/responds to conflicting viewpoints maps to argumentative SE 8.8E 'identifying and explaining the counter argument.'
Delineate/evaluate the argument, reasoning, and evidence maps to argumentative SE 8.8E (identifying claim, analyzing argument, counterargument).
Evaluating claims/evidence overlaps Response SE 8.6J 'defend or challenge the authors' claims using relevant text evidence.'
Analyzing two texts with conflicting information on the same topic maps to genre-neutral Response SE 8.6B 'comparing sources within and across genres.'

unmatched.why: Comparing a text to its audio/video/live version has no TEKS reading analogue; multimodal SE 6.8F addresses characteristics of multimodal texts, not cross-medium comparison.
Analyzing how a key individual/event/idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated has no clean TEKS SE; only generic informational-structure coverage exists.
Integrating information across media/formats (visual, quantitative) has no TEKS reading analogue; 6.8F covers multimodal characteristics, not integration.
Range/text-complexity proficiency standard with no skill content and (unlike RL.6.10) no connections clause; no TEKS equivalent.
Comparing a written work to its filmed/staged/multimedia version has no TEKS reading analogue for cross-medium comparison.
Analyzing interactions between individuals, events, and ideas has no clean TEKS SE beyond generic informational structure.
Comparing a text to an audio/video/multimedia version has no TEKS cross-medium-comparison SE.
Range/text-complexity proficiency standard with no discrete skill and no TEKS equivalent.
Evaluating fidelity of a filmed/live production to the text has no TEKS cross-medium-comparison analogue.
Analyzing how modern fiction alludes to myths/traditional/religious texts has no clean TEKS SE; genre-knowledge SE 8.8A is about recognizing genres, not allusion analysis.
Analyzing connections/distinctions among individuals, ideas, events (comparisons, analogies, categories) has no clean TEKS SE beyond generic informational structure.
Evaluating advantages/disadvantages of different mediums for presenting a topic has no TEKS cross-medium-evaluation analogue.
Range/text-complexity proficiency standard with no discrete skill and no TEKS equivalent.
